parameters.url: ={{ (() => { const lookbackDays = 180; const end = new Date(); const start = new Date(end); start.setDate(start.getDate() - lookbackDays); const fmt = (d) => `${d.getFullYear()}-${String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')}-${String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')}`; return `https://data-api.ecb.europa.eu/service/data/EXR/D.BRL.EUR.SP00.A?startPeriod=${fmt(start)}&endPeriod=${fmt(end)}&detail=dataonly&format=csvdata`; })() }}

parameters.text: ={{ JSON.stringify({ quote_date: $json.quote_date, fx_rate_brl_per_eur: $json.fx_rate_brl_per_eur, change_vs_previous: $json.change_vs_previous, change_pct_vs_previous: $json.change_pct_vs_previous, fx_stats_lookback: $json.fx_stats_lookback, movement_label: $json.movement_label, rule_based_signals: $json.signals, fallback_summary_en: $json.summary_en }, null, 2) }}

messageValues.message:
You are interpreting ECB EUR/BRL exchange rate data for a Lavazza coffee intelligence system. EUR/BRL rate affects the cost of Brazilian coffee denominated in euros. Describe what is happening factually — do not judge if it is good or bad.

Output format:
{
  "movement_label": "...",
  "signals": ["..."],
  "summary_en": "..."
}

movement_label options: eur_strengthening|eur_weakening|eur_stable|eur_volatile
Rules:
- signals: max 5 short English strings describing observable facts
- DO NOT assign positive/negative valence — just describe what is happening
- movement_label: one concise label describing the current situation
- summary_en: 1-2 English sentences, factual only
- return JSON only, no extra text
